2606.27286.title: Simulation-based inference for rapid Bayesian parameter estimation in epidemiological models: a comparison with MCMC
2606.27286.paper_summary: Mechanistic epidemiological models are widely used to support infectious disease forecasting and public-health decision making. Bayesian calibration of such models is commonly performed using Markov chain Monte Carlo (MCMC), which can become computationally expensive for high-dimensional nonlinear systems and repeated near-real-time analyses. Here, we investigate simulation-based inference (SBI) using neural posterior estimation as a scalable alternative for Bayesian calibration of a mechanistic SECIR epidemiological model using COVID-19 intensive care unit (ICU) occupancy data from Germany during 2020. We compared SBI and MCMC across multiple epidemic phases using both 31-day inference windows and a substantially more challenging 201-day reconstruction problem involving multiple transmission change points. Posterior agreement was evaluated quantitatively using Wasserstein distances and Kullback-Leibler divergences together with posterior predictive checks. Across the 31-day windows, SBI recovered posterior distributions in strong agreement with MCMC while accurately reproducing observed ICU trajectories. In the 201-day setting, SBI preserved the dominant posterior structure despite increased uncertainty. SBI, by combining CPU and GPU resources, substantially reduced computational runtime compared with MCMC, which was restricted to running on CPUs. Whereas MCMC required approximately 1000 seconds for the 31-day inference problems, SBI achieved comparable posterior and predictive performance in approximately 60-70 seconds on a single GPU. For the 201-day inference problem, SBI required an average of 157 seconds, while the MCMC runs took over 19,000 seconds. Our results demonstrate that SBI provides a rapid and computationally efficient framework for Bayesian calibration of mechanistic epidemiological models, supporting repeated near-real-time inference and rapid outbreak analysis.
2606.27286.paper_summary_zh: 機制流行病學模型被廣泛用於支持傳染病預測和公共衛生決策。這類模型的貝葉斯校準通常使用馬爾可夫鏈蒙特卡羅（MCMC）進行，對於高維非線性系統和重複的近實時分析來說，計算成本可能非常高。在此，我們使用神經後驗估計進行基於模擬的推斷（SBI），作為一種可擴展的替代方案，對2020年德國COVID-19重症監護病房（ICU）佔用數據的機制SECIR流行病學模型進行貝葉斯校準。我們在多個流行病階段比較了SBI和MCMC，使用31天的推斷窗口以及涉及多個傳播變化點的更具挑戰性的201天重建問題。後驗一致性通過使用Wasserstein距離和Kullback-Leibler散度以及後驗預測檢查進行了定量評估。在31天的窗口中，SBI恢復的後驗分佈與MCMC高度一致，同時準確再現了觀察到的ICU軌跡。在201天的設置中，儘管不確定性增加，SBI仍保留了主要的後驗結構。SBI通過結合CPU和GPU資源，顯著減少了計算運行時間，而MCMC則限制於在CPU上運行。對於31天的推斷問題，MCMC大約需要1000秒，而SBI在單個GPU上大約只需60-70秒即可達到可比的後驗和預測性能。對於201天的推斷問題，SBI平均需要157秒，而MCMC運行則超過19000秒。我們的結果表明，SBI提供了一種快速且計算高效的框架，用於機制流行病學模型的貝葉斯校準，支持重複的近實時推斷和快速疫情分析。
2606.27286.author: Alina Bazarova et.al.
2606.27286.authors: Alina Bazarova, Johann Fredrik Jadebeck, Henrik Zunker, Carolina J. Klett-Tammen, Torben Heinsohn, Wolfgang Wiechert, Katharina Noeh, Stefan Kesselheim
